channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Who needs cup final victory most - Arteta or Guardiola?
item.description: Premier League leaders Arsenal face title rivals Manchester City in the EFL Cup final on Sunday, but how important could the result prove in title race?

item.title: Newcastle-Sunderland halted after discrimination reports
item.description: Referee Anthony Taylor stopped the Premier League match in line with the competition's on-field anti-discrimination protocol.

item.title: Six touches in the box, six goals - the epitome of clinical?
item.description: Is QPR's 6-1 rout of Portsmouth - in which they only had six touches in the opposition's penalty area - the most clinical performance ever?

item.title: Spurs v Forest - the 'disarray derby' key to survival hopes
item.description: Chaos clubs Tottenham Hotspur and Nottingham Forest are battling to survive in the Premier League - and face each other on Sunday.

item.title: Jorginho's criticism of Chappell Roan over daughter's tears goes viral
item.description: Ex-Chelsea and Arsenal midfielder Jorginho goes viral with a social media post alleging singer Chappell Roan's security guard made his daughter cry.

item.title: Beto leads Everton to victory over disjointed Chelsea
item.description: Beto scores twice for Everton to win 3-0 and condemn Chelsea to their fourth loss in a row across all competitions.

item.title: Fulham come from behind to beat Burnley and boost European hopes
item.description: Fulham boost their European hopes as they fought back to beat Premier League strugglers Burnley at Craven Cottage.

item.title: Welbeck scores twice as Brighton beat Liverpool
item.description: Danny Welbeck scores his 11th and 12th Premier League goals of the season, as Brighton & Hove Albion inflict more misery on Liverpool.

item.title: Brentford held to goalless draw at Leeds
item.description: Leeds United play out a goalless draw against Brentford at Elland Road seeing themmove four points clear of the Premier League's bottom three.

item.title: Are Arsenal looking stronger? Football Focus previews Carabao Cup final
item.description: Football Focus pundits Nedum Onuoha and Dion Dublin preview Sunday's Carabao Cup final as Arsenal take on Manchester City.

item.title: Should 'inexperienced' Chelsea ditch youth project?
item.description: Match of the Day pundits Alan Shearer and Wayne Rooney insist Chelsea need to pivot away from their transfer strategy focused soley on purchasing younger players, believing their inexperienced squad may be the cause for their inconsistent form.

item.title: How Brighton dominated 'messy' Liverpool
item.description: Match of the Day pundits Wayne Rooney and Alan Shearer break down what went wrong for Virgil van Dijk and Liverpool as they "absolutely bullied" by Brighton.

item.title: Shaw hat-trick helps Man City to convincing win against Spurs
item.description: Bunny Shaw scores a record-breaking hat-trick inside 21 minutes as Manchester City beat Tottenham Hotspur 5-2.

item.title: Successive World Cup would 'surpass all dreams' - Davies
item.description: Defender Ben Davies says Wales reaching consecutive World Cup finals would "surpass all dreams".

item.title: 'They think I just go home and make TikToks - it's not true'
item.description: Leicester forward Alisha Lehmann is the most-followed female footballer in the world and says she has learned to cope.

item.title: Outrun and in decline - are Rosenior's Chelsea near crisis point?
item.description: Chelsea enter freefall after another defeat - 3-0 away at Everton - and have two weeks over the international break to stew on a damaging 10 days.

item.title: 'If Liverpool play like that at PSG it could be 10-0'
item.description: Arne Slot needs answers quickly or Liverpool's unwanted records may no longer be his only worry.

item.title: The race no-one wants to win - are Everton serious top-five contenders?
item.description: The fight for Champions League football appears to be turning into one no-one wants to win, but have Everton snuck into the race?

item.title: How to follow Italy v Northern Ireland across BBC Sport NI
item.description: Northern Ireland's crucial World Cup play-off semi-final against Italy is almost upon us - and you can keep up with all the build-up and action from Bergamo across the BBC.

item.title: How Jimenez thrives from stuttering penalty style
item.description: BBC Sport looks at how Raul Jimenez has become the Premier League penalty king.

item.title: 'It's not Allan, it's Arne' - Tudor explains viral moment
item.description: Tottenham boss Igor Tudor clears up the viral moment where it was thought he mistakenly hugged someone thinking it was Liverpool manager Arne Slot.

item.title: 'Not just goals, he's a great connecter' - should Welbeck be in England squad?
item.description: Danny Welbeck scores two goals against Liverpool on Saturday to take his tally for the season to 12 - one day after he was left out of Thomas Tuchel's England squad.

item.title: Kelly back to 'best version' before Arsenal's crucial week
item.description: How "the best version of Chloe Kelly" helped Arsenal to an emphatic WSL victory over West Ham.

item.title: Why McInnes thinks 'huge underdogs' Hearts will get stronger
item.description: Hearts head coach Derek McInnes says they "will get stronger" in the final seven games. With players returning to bolster his squad, you can see why.

item.title: 'Not good enough' - Rosenior rues Chelsea mistakes
item.description: Chelsea manager Liam Rosenior says he is disappointed in his side's performance against Everton after they "gifted goals" to the opposition.

item.title: Kane & Olise score as Bayern beat Union Berlin
item.description: Bayern Munich put four past Union Berlin, including a goal from Harry Kane that moves him within 10 of the Bundesliga single-season goals record.

item.title: Kelly scores twice as Arsenal thrash West Ham
item.description: Watch highlights as Chloe Kelly scores twice as Arsenal beat West Ham 5-0 in the Women's Super League at Emirates Stadium.

item.title: Liverpool struggling because of lack of players - Slot
item.description: Liverpool boss Arne Slot says injuries and lack of options in his squad is one of the reasons they are "struggling" following their 2-1 defeat by Brighton & Hove Albion, with injury to Hugo Ekitike adding to the absences of Mohamed Salah and Alexander Isak.

item.title: Man Utd snatch victory with late Malard winner against Everton
item.description: Watch highlights as Melvine Malard's 94th-minute winner snatches victory for Manchester United against Everton to send them back into second place in the Women's Super League table.

item.title: Officiating getting worse because of VAR - Shearer
item.description: Pundits discuss the use of VAR, saying refereeing standards are "only getting worse" because of it.

item.title: Arsenal step up Franca interest - Sunday's gossip
item.description: Roma's Wesley Franca emerges as a target for Arsenal, Liverpool and Chelsea keen on Real Madrid midfielder Eduardo Camavinga while Marcus Rashford faces uncertain Barcelona future.

item.title: Why is Northern Ireland's World Cup play-off in Bergamo?
item.description: Italy host Northern Ireland in a World Cup play-off on Thursday, but why is the game being played in Bergamo and not in Milan or Rome.

item.title: Late London City equaliser ends Chelsea's winning run
item.description: Isobel Goodwin scores a late equaliser for London City Lionesses and ends Chelsea's five-game winning run.

item.title: Welbeck is irreplaceable for Brighton - Hurzeler
item.description: Brighton and Hove Albion manager Fabian Hurzeler speaks about Danny Welbeck's "remarkable season" for Brighton after his performance in their 2-1 win over Liverpool.

item.title: 'The referee got it wrong' - Pundits disagree on Amad penalty claim
item.description: Football Focus' Dion Dublin says he thinks "the referee got it wrong" and Manchester United should have been awarded a penalty when Amad Diallo appeared to be pulled back inside the box by Adrien Truffert.

item.title: Maguire sent off as Bournemouth fight back to draw with Man Utd
item.description: Despite taking the lead twice, Manchester United are held to a 2-2 draw at Bournemouth and have Harry Maguire sent off as they drop points in the race for a top-four finish in the Premier League.

item.title: I could play a few more years - Milner
item.description: Brighton's 40-year-old midfielder James Milner says he's capable of playing for a "few more years".

item.title: Shaw hat-trick heroics 'like watching video game'
item.description: Khadija Shaw's performance against Tottenham was "like watching a video game", says team-mate Sam Coffey after latest three-goal display.

item.title: Klopp, small shinpads and whether he really is boring - Milner in his own words
item.description: James Milner - the Premier League's record appearance holder, speaks to reporter Kelly Somers about his career - including a debut goal for hometown club Leeds, a dramatic Champions League semi-final comeback against Barcelona and returning from nine months out injured.

item.title: 'It was chaos' - referee frustrates Carrick as Bournemouth hold Man Utd
item.description: Manchester United manager Michael Carrick was left frustrated by what he considered to be inconsistent refereeing decisions during his side's 2-2 draw at Bournemouth.

item.title: 'There isn't much to discuss' - Iraola unfazed by penalty decisions
item.description: Bournemouth manager Andoni Iraola says "there isn't much to discuss" when asked about the controversial penalty decisions during his sides 2-2 with Manchester United, a draw which takes Bournemouth's unbeaten run in the Premier League to 11 matches, equalling a club record.

item.title: Five-star Leipzig thrash Hoffenheim to boost Champions League hopes
item.description: RB Leipzig thrash Hoffenheim 5-0 in the Bundesliga to secure a vital win in the race to finish in the top four and qualify for next season's Champions League.

item.title: 'I'd love her to be here' - Fernandez on Putellas friendship and London City
item.description: London City Lionesses and Spain defender Jana Fernandez talks to Alex Scott about adjusting to life in the WSL after moving from Barcelona, in a new episode of Eat & Yap which you can watch in full on BBC iPlayer.

item.title: How clubs are developing football stars of the future
item.description: BBC Sport takes a trip to Malta to see an under-10s tournament featuring Benfica and Newcastle United.

item.title: History made as Messi hits 900 goals
item.description: Lionel Messi's scores his 900th goal in Inter Miami's match against Nashville SC in the Concacaf Champions Cup. The 38-year-old joins Cristiano Ronaldo in reaching the milestone. Watch some of his greatest World Cup goals.

item.title: Potter, 19, scores on first start as Chelsea beat Brighton
item.description: Nineteen-year-old Lexi Potter scores the winner on her first Chelsea start as the hosts beat Brighton 2-1 in the WSL.

item.title: One final experiment? Unpicking Tuchel's latest England squad
item.description: Thomas Tuchel's expanded England squad offers hope to forgotten men but shows problems before the World Cup, writes Phil McNulty.

item.title: My plan to overhaul academy system and help young players
item.description: Tony Pulis on why more should be done to help youngsters in the academy system, especially the majority who do not make it as professionals.

item.title: The 15-year-old Man Utd prodigy still too young to play senior football
item.description: At 15, Manchester United's JJ Gabriel is too young to play in the Premier League but he is already showing clear signs he is a huge talent.

item.title: Four English teams out in 24 hours - what happened and why?
item.description: With just two out of six Premier League teams progressing to the Champions League quarter-finals, what went wrong for the English sides?

item.title: Credit to Tudor - is he turning around Spurs side who looked lost?
item.description: Tottenham can take encouragement in their battle for Premier League survival despite a Champions League exit, writes Phil McNulty.

item.title: Afcon final chaos - key questions answered
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team look at why the 2025 Afcon final result has been overturned - and what it means.

item.title: All February and March's done deals, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: When can Arsenal win the Premier League?
item.description: Arsenal are nine points clear at the top of the Premier League, but when can they officially win the title?

item.title: What happens when a manager gets suspended?
item.description: Ever wondered what a touchline ban entails? BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explain what punishment managers actually face.

item.title: All you need to know about 2027 Women's World Cup
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team take a look at everything you need to know for the 2027 Women's World Cup.

item.title: Which club has the most red cards in a Premier League season?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything looks at the record for most red cards in a Premier League season

item.title: What is St Totteringham's Day and when could it happen?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains what St Totteringham's Day is and what its origins are

item.title: VAR vs TMO – what are the key differences?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything looks at the key differences between football's video assistant referees and rugby union's use of television match officials.

item.title: Who has the most Premier League appearances?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything Team look at which players have the highest total Premier League appearances.

item.title: Laporta re-elected Barcelona president for fourth term
item.description: Joan Laporta vows Barcelona will be "unstoppable" as he is re-elected club president for a further five years.

item.title: Cole appointed manager of Serie B side Cesena
item.description: Former Arsenal and Chelsea defender Ashley Cole appointed manager of Serie B side Cesena until the end of the season.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.

item.title: Napoli 'smell blood' & target Premier League's discarded stars
item.description: After Scott McTominay was a hit, Napoli went back to the Premier League for Kevin de Bruyne, could more players follow them to Serie A?

item.title: When River Plate icon Diaz took over fourth-tier Oxford
item.description: Twenty years on from Ramon Diaz's surprising appointment at Oxford United, BBC Sport looks at what happened and how it went.
